Personal workstream · not engineering

Plan the warm-intro outreach batch

Turn a loose set of outreach targets into a deliberate, ordered plan — who to reach, in what order, and through which mutual connection — so the next few weeks of conversations are intentional rather than ad hoc.

The outcome
A written sequence the owner can work down by hand — with cadence and a per-target angle.
Scope
A plan, not an automated send. No code, no system change.
Inputs
A large network + a short target list: a couple of companies, a couple of roles, one or two names.
Still open
2 judgment calls — the plan isn't final until they're settled.
01

What I'm planning

A personal plan to sequence a batch of warm-intro outreach across the owner's professional network — deciding who to reach, in what order, and through which mutual — so the next few weeks of conversations are deliberate, not ad hoc.

  1. 1For each target, find the warmest path in — a shared employer, a school overlap, or a strong mutual connection.
  2. 2Decide ask-for-intro vs. reach directly for each one.
  3. 3Stage the asks so no single well-connected person is leaned on for five intros in one week.
  4. 4Write it down with a rough cadence (who this week, who next) and a note on the angle for each, so the outreach reads as personal and considered.
an engineering task an automated send a code or system change
02

Still to decide

Two genuine judgment calls are still open — the sequencing approach isn't settled until they are.

Open

Batch the asks, or keep them separate?

When one well-connected mutual could make several intros, send one combined ask — or keep each separate so every intro feels considered rather than transactional?

One "could you intro me to these two?" vs Separate, one at a time
Open

How warm before going direct?

How warm does a connection have to be before reaching out directly beats asking for an intro? The threshold is a judgment call the owner hasn't pinned down.

Reach directly vs Ask for an intro
A personal plan to work down by hand · 2 open questions to settle first
